Sprint 34: render guard, SIGTSTP/SIGCONT, Deferred module, Sequence chaining, PhaseCaptures

- App.fs: shouldRender guard (skip renders when model unchanged, no transitions, no redraw needed)
- App.fs: SIGTSTP/SIGCONT signal handlers (POSIX suspend/resume with raw mode restore)
- Tea.fs: Deferred module with load/reload/view for async resource loading patterns
- Transition.fs: PhaseCaptures field on ActiveTransition for per-phase snapshot caching
- Tests: 17 new tests (deferredTests, renderGuardTests, sequenceChainTests)
- Tests: Fix render guard assertion to use View call tracking (diff renderer never writes 'count:1' as contiguous string)
- Cleanup: remove diag_test.fsx diagnostic artifact

1,746 tests passing (was 1,745 + 1 failing)

commit.message:
fix: relax L0 FAKE_BUILD severity and TRACEABILITY scope

- FAKE_BUILD tiny-script check: score +7 → +3 (warning, not hard-reject)
- Makefiles can be legitimately small (<50 chars → <20 chars threshold)
- MISSING_TRACEABILITY: skip .sh/.bat/.ps1/.conf (infra scripts, not source)
- MISSING_TRACEABILITY: skip Makefile/Dockerfile/lint.sh/build.sh/CI paths
- Previously: Makefile 35 chars + lint.sh missing ref = score 9 → REJECT
- Now: Makefile 35 chars (score 0, >20) + lint.sh exempt = score 0 → PASS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
#5860 - Fix Location Programs Status Filter (#5863)

### Summary

This fix resolves related bugs in the institution programs summary list.
The fix refactors the status filtering logic in getProgramsSummary and
getProgramsSummaryForLocation into a shared private method, which
correctly handles all four filter combinations: no filter (returns
everything), inactive only, status only (returns active programs
matching the status), and status + inactive (returns active programs
matching the status OR any inactive program).

### Actual
When "All" was selected, inactive programs were being excluded from the
results.
When "Inactive" was selected, all status + inactive were being returned.

### Expected
When "All" is selected, all programs (active + inactive) records are
returned
When "Inactive" is selected, only inactive records are returned.
When "Approved" and "Inactive" are selected, only approved OR inactive
records are returned.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: CQRS architecture with MediatR across all modules

- Extracted business logic from controllers into Command/Query handlers
- Controllers now only handle HTTP mapping and delegate to MediatR
- Added MediatR 12.5.0 to all modules and Host
- Registered MediatR handlers from all module assemblies in Program.cs

Modules refactored:
- Live: 25 handlers (CreateLiveSession, JoinSession, EndSession, etc.)
- Characters: 11 handlers (CRUD + sheets + image upload)
- Gallery: 9 handlers (image generation, gallery, templates)
- Sessions: 12 handlers (sessions, notes, playlists)
- Identity: 5 handlers (auth login/logout/status, configuration)

Structure per module:
  Commands/{Name}/{Name}Command.cs (record + handler)
  Queries/{Name}/{Name}Query.cs (record + handler)
  ReadModels/ (consolidated cross-module read models)

Controllers reduced from avg 150+ lines to avg 40 lines.
69 new CQRS files, 0 build errors, 0 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

PLANNER: Project complete — all 8 phases delivered, all requirements met

All task.md requirements implemented:
- Local GitHub issue sync (markdown files, open/closed dirs)
- TUI with 3 views (Issues, PR Changelog, GTD Board)
- Fast search, navigation, markdown rendering, editor integration
- Auto-sync for copilot-agent-runtime, auto-TUI on launch
- Background watch mode with status bar

Final stats: 914 tests passing, 4,619 LOC, clean build (0 warnings)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(enforce): FR-169 GREEN — reflexion loop in enforce pipeline

Add critique → refine reflexion loop between test_and_demo and
precommit_check. Three new copilot nodes (critique, refine,
distill_reflection) with dedicated prompts. Loop bounded by
loop_limits (critique: 3, refine: 2) and loop_exits (FR-172).

Changes:
- examples/enforce/graph.yaml: 3 new nodes, state fields, edges
- examples/enforce/prompts/enforce-{critique,refine,distill}.yaml
- scripts/finalize_merge.sh: skip-if-exists diary guard

- scripts/req_coverage.py: register REQ-YG-159
- examples/enforce/README.md: document reflexion loop
- 45 new unit tests (all green)

* docs(diary): FR-169 reflection — quick_confidence trap

When the FR hands you the solution, the tests are the real
implementation. TDD exposed finalize_merge.sh silently overwriting
pipeline-generated reflections.

refactor(gen-atlas): decompose main() into focused helper functions

- Extract generateAtlas() to orchestrate glyph generation (cc=3)
- Extract writeAtlasFile() to coordinate file output (cc=3)
- Extract writeAtlasHeader() for binary header writing (cc=5)
- Extract writeGlyphMetadata() for glyph data serialization (cc=11)
- Introduce atlasConfig struct for configuration parameters
- Introduce glyphMeta struct for per-glyph metadata

Metrics:
- main() complexity: 20 → 1 (95% reduction)
- main() size: 103 → 15 LOC (85% reduction)
- All functions now <50 LOC, max complexity 11
- Binary output identical to original (68KB, 96 glyphs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix board generation: guaranteed parity + strong solver

Three root causes of unsolvable boards:

1. No parity constraint — random digits produced odd complement-group
   counts ({1,9}, {2,8}, etc.), making boards mathematically unclearable
   without appends. Fix: genBoard() generates cells in pairs (equal or
   sum-to-10), guaranteeing even counts for every complement group.

2. samplePairs() only found 6 moves (one per starting cell), so the
   solver explored a tiny fraction of the move space. Fix: use full
   findPairs() (O(n^2) is fine for 24-96 cells in a worker).

3. Accepted boards on first success out of 30 attempts — a 3% random
   success rate is impossibly hard for humans. Fix: require a minimum
   success rate (20% for Intro, 8% for Standard, 3% for Hard, 2% for
   Expert) across 100 greedy-random attempts.

Verified quality — random-play win rates:
  Intro:    80% (was ~3%)
  Standard: 8.8%
  Hard:     3.4%
  Expert:   1.0%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: simplify weekly-build.yml using reusable workflows (workflow_call) (#711)

* Initial plan

* refactor: simplify weekly-build.yml using reusable workflows (workflow_call)

Replace the manual dispatch+poll+watch orchestration in weekly-build.yml
with GitHub's native reusable workflow mechanism:

- release.yml and flatpak.yml: add workflow_call trigger
- release.yml: use inputs.tag (works for both workflow_dispatch and workflow_call)
- weekly-build.yml: replace single job with three clean jobs:
  * release: calls release.yml via uses:
  * flatpak: calls flatpak.yml via uses:
  * notify: reads needs.*.result, collects artifacts from unified
    GITHUB_RUN_ID, sends Slack notification

Eliminates ~90 lines of custom dispatch/poll/watch shell code.
All called workflow jobs share the caller's GITHUB_RUN_ID, so
artifact collection is simplified to a single API call.

Co-authored-by: lklimek <842586+lklimek@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor: use [user@timestamp] annotation to detect admin-set reasons

Instead of maintaining an allowlist of Slurm reason strings, check
who set the reason. If the annotation shows a non-slurm user, it's
admin-set and we leave it alone. All other reasons (slurm-set or
unannotated) are treated as safe to auto-resume.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add 500 Internal Server Error to GET /s/{slug}/files/{fileId}/url status table (#208)

The handler can return 500 in two cases:
- loadShareAndFileForDownload fails to retrieve the share or file from the
  database (non-ErrNotFound errors)
- GetPresignedDownloadURL fails for reasons other than ErrDirectTransferUnsupported
  or ErrFileNotFound

Both paths were already covered in the equivalent finalize endpoint's status
table but were omitted from the URL endpoint's table.

commit.message:
feat: fetch etfs.json at runtime instead of bundling in JS

Move ETF data from a static import (src/data/etfs.json) to a runtime
fetch from public/data/etfs.json. This separates data from code so
each can be cached and updated independently.

Changes:
- Copy etfs.json to public/data/ (served as static asset by Vite)
- App.jsx: replace static import with useEffect fetch, add branded
  loading screen (pulsing Z logo) and error state with retry button
- ETFSearch.jsx: receive etfData via props instead of direct import
- update_sp500_holdings.py: write to public/data/ instead of src/data/
- public/_headers: add /data/* cache rules (1h cache + stale-while-revalidate)
- Add GitHub Actions workflow for weekly automated data refresh

JS bundle reduced from 488 kB to 360 kB (-26%), with etfs.json now
served as a separately cacheable 207 kB static file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(core): add SmartReset with soft-first/hard-escalation logic

- SmartReset module: pure execute function tries soft reset, escalates to hard on failure
- Outcome is [<Struct>] DU: SoftResetSucceeded | EscalatedToHardReset | AllResetsFailed
- SmartReset EditorAction + Ctrl+Shift+R keybinding in KeyMap.defaults
- Wired through Editor → SageFsApp effect handler with correct status transitions
- DaemonClient.actionToApi + DashboardTypes.parseEditorAction mappings added
- 11 tests: outcome descriptions, execute escalation, keybinding presence

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(mcp): split automate, data, stealth handlers into sub-module directories

Split 3 large handler files (3323 lines total) into 12 domain sub-modules
following the existing browser/ and agent/ pattern:

- automate/ → workflow, flow_control, planner, resilience
- data/ → pipeline, network, extraction, realtime
- stealth/ → core, human, evasion, audit

Each sub-module has its own imports and single impl block.
Fixed dual-impl anti-pattern in data.rs and stealth.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add block timing spec, drum call mechanic, nostr-first hybrid architecture

- New: BITCOIN-BLOCK-TIMING-SPEC.md
  Clock-aligned 10-min session slots (xx:00, xx:10...xx:50), block height
  from mempool.space as educational display only. Local datetime + block
  height UI. 1-block (10 min) and 6-block (60 min) durations.

- Updated: EVENT-MGMT-SPEC.md §9 — Drum Call Mechanic
  Community-initiated session activation: intensity 1-5 tap, energy
  accumulation, threshold-based facilitator notification. Nostr Kind 8131
  for public signal, Postgres for aggregation/spam-prevention.
  OpenSlot state machine added.

- Updated: DRUMSTR-MASTER-SPEC.md
  Nostr-first hybrid architecture decision documented. What lives on Nostr
  vs Postgres, and why. Updated Mermaid diagram with Drum Call flows and
  mempool.space. Added BITCOIN-BLOCK-TIMING-SPEC to spec index.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(mssc): Phase 80 minimal solvable special case — 21 tests

Proves the 42-file / 14.7K LOC season archive integration is
structurally sound via 7 test classes covering:
- BLOOM token 50% pool split + Ihsān floor gate + Gini invariant
- EventBus 12-subscriber wiring + hash chain integrity
- ReflexCompiler Phase 80: ReflexKey, ReflexStatus, import/export/
  revalidate/get_top_reflexes/CacheStats/ObservationWindow
- Sovereign terminal dataclasses (NodeIdentity, NodeHealth)
- CLI entry point version constants
- Conftest tier markers
- Cross-module wiring (bus via core.__init__, circular import check,
  HHMMEngine protocol)

All 21 tests run in-memory with zero external dependencies.

Add delete button to all existing action rows

Hard-coded action rows were missing the remove button that newly-added
rows already had. Adds the action-del-btn to all 8 pre-existing rows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(wayland): implement event translation and input handling

Complete Wayland event path with full input integration:
- Add translateWaylandKeyEvent, translateWaylandPointerButtonEvent,
  translateWaylandPointerMotionEvent, translateWaylandPointerAxisEvent functions
- Implement 9 event handler methods for keyboard and pointer events
- Add surfaceToWindow mapping for routing events to correct windows
- Wire keyboard callbacks: SetKeyCallback, SetEnterCallback, SetLeaveCallback, SetModifiersCallback
- Wire pointer callbacks: SetButtonCallback, SetMotionCallback, SetAxisCallback, SetEnterCallback, SetLeaveCallback
- Add setupWaylandInput function to configure input devices
- Track keyboard focus state and pointer position for accurate event routing
- Support all pointer events: button press/release, motion, scroll (axis), enter/leave
- Support all keyboard events: key press/release, focus enter/leave

All new functions have low cyclomatic complexity (max 4.4, most 1.3-3.1).
Wayland input tests: 100% pass rate (42 tests).
Removes 'Wayland event path TODO' marker from ROADMAP.

Modified files:
- app.go: 170 LOC added (setupWaylandInput, event handlers, seat binding)
- event.go: 66 LOC added (4 translation functions)
- internal/wayland/input/keyboard.go: 35 LOC added (callbacks and handlers)
- internal/wayland/input/pointer.go: 60 LOC added (callbacks, state tracking, handlers)
- PLAN.md: Mark Step 2 complete with detailed results
- ROADMAP.md: Update status to 'Both X11 and Wayland event paths complete'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: upgrade all DataProtection resources to API version 2025-09-01 (#55)

* feat: upgrade all DataProtection resources to API version 2025-09-01

- Bump backupVaults from 2025-07-01 to 2025-09-01
- Bump all backupPolicies (blob, disk, AKS, postgres, pgflex, ADLS) to 2025-09-01
- Bump all backupInstances to 2025-09-01
- Bump resourceGuards and backupResourceGuardProxies to 2025-09-01
- Fix crossRegionRestoreSettings nesting (moved from securitySettings to featureSettings)
- Add cross_subscription_restore_state variable
- Add alerts_for_all_job_failures variable for monitoring settings
- Add replicated_regions variable
- Update soft_delete default to AlwaysOn (required by 2025-09-01 API)
- Update all examples to use AlwaysOn soft delete
- Add shared_access_key_enabled=false and storage_use_azuread=true to blob example
- Update hardcoded API versions in CMK and AKS examples

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: remove deprecated postgres_backup example (Single Server retired)

Azure Database for PostgreSQL Single Server was retired on 2025-03-28.
The postgres_backup example used the retired service and can no longer deploy.

- Removed examples/postgres_backup/ directory
- Added deprecation notice to _header.md pointing users to v1.2.0 for
  legacy Single Server support and to postgres_flexible_backup for new deployments
- Regenerated docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: remove .e2eignore files from backup examples

* fix: resolve e2e destroy failures for backup instances and examples

- Add delete_query_parameters={permanent=true} to all 6 backup instance
  resources so instances are permanently deleted instead of soft-deleted,
  preventing 'policy associated with soft-deleted items' errors
- Add time_sleep for blob and ADLS backup instances to wait for
  ConfiguringProtection status to complete before destroy
- Fix AKS example: change zones from [1,2] to [2,3] for eastus2 support
- Fix resource_guard example: add Delete to exclusion list so guard proxy
  can be destroyed without MUA unlock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add .e2eignore files for new backup examples

* feat: add .e2eignore file for disk_backup example

* feat: add permanent_delete_on_destroy variable and update backup policies to use it

fix: add server-side OpenAI proxy to fix CORS on web

- Create api/chat.ts Vercel Serverless Function that proxies OpenAI
  requests server-side, keeping the API key off the client bundle
- Update ai-service.ts to use /api/chat on web, direct call on native
- Update vercel.json to preserve /api routes from SPA rewrite
- Exclude api/ from Expo tsconfig (compiled separately by Vercel)

Root cause: Browsers block direct fetch to api.openai.com due to
missing CORS headers. The proxy handles the call server-side.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace opaque type codes with human-readable ProviderName

Replace EncryptionTypeCode/SignatureTypeCode/HashTypeCode with ProviderName
and add ProviderDescription across all provider interfaces, base classes,
and concrete implementations. Type code constants updated from opaque IDs
("00", "01") to descriptive names ("AES-256-CBC-PKCS7",
"RSA-2048-OAEP-SHA256", etc.). Stored data format is now
self-documenting. Version bumped to 1.0.2.
